title: Entrepreneurship Basics
description: Validate and launch your business idea with confidence — without quitting your job before you are ready.

modules.title: From Idea to Opportunity
modules.description: Learn to evaluate whether your idea is worth pursuing.

lessons.title: Problem–Solution Fit
lessons.content:
# Problem–Solution Fit

The biggest reason startups fail is building something nobody wants. Problem–solution fit is how you avoid that.

## Step 1: Define the Problem Precisely
- Who has this problem? (Be specific — "small restaurant owners in suburban areas", not "businesses".)
- How painful is it? (1–10 scale — is it a vitamin or a painkiller?)
- How do they currently solve it?
- What are the flaws in that current solution?

## Step 2: Test Your Solution Hypothesis
Before building anything:
- Conduct 10–15 customer discovery interviews.
- Show a simple mockup or landing page — measure interest.
- Offer to solve the problem manually first (the "concierge MVP").

## What You Are Listening For
- Do people get excited when you describe the solution?
- Would they pay for it today?
- Have they already tried to solve this themselves?

## Red Flags
- "That is interesting" (but they do not ask how to sign up).
- No one has the problem except you.

modules.title: Starting Lean
modules.description: Build the smallest version of your business that generates real learning.

lessons.title: The Minimum Viable Product
lessons.content:
# The Minimum Viable Product (MVP)

An MVP is the smallest thing you can build to test your most important assumption.

## What an MVP Is Not
- A half-finished product.
- A product with every feature you planned.

## MVP Types

| Type | Example |
|---|---|
| Landing page | Describe the product, collect emails, measure interest |
| Concierge | Do the service manually before automating |
| Wizard of Oz | Simulate the tech behind the scenes |
| Pre-sale | Sell before building to validate demand |
| Prototype | Clickable mockup tested with real users |

## The Build–Measure–Learn Loop
1. **Build** the smallest testable thing.
2. **Measure** a specific metric (sign-ups, retention, revenue).
3. **Learn** — does the data support your hypothesis? Pivot or persevere.

## Speed Is the Goal
The faster you complete the loop, the faster you learn — and the less money you waste.

lessons.title: Basic Business Finance for Founders
lessons.content:
# Basic Business Finance for Founders

You do not need an accounting degree, but you do need to understand these numbers.

## The Three Core Metrics

### Revenue
Money coming in. Know your monthly recurring revenue (MRR) if you have subscriptions.

### Costs
- **Fixed costs** — the same every month regardless of sales (rent, software subscriptions, salaries).
- **Variable costs** — change with sales volume (materials, transaction fees, shipping).

### Profit / Loss
`Profit = Revenue − Total Costs`
If this number is negative, you are burning cash. How many months can you sustain that?

## Break-Even Analysis
At what revenue level do your total costs equal your total revenue? This is your break-even point.

## Cash Flow vs. Profit
You can be profitable on paper and still run out of cash. Understand *when* money arrives and *when* bills are due.

## Key Rule
Price for profit from day one — raising prices later is far harder than setting them right at the start.
